title: 'Sinners' Wins 4 Oscars After Record-Breaking 16 Nominations
text: Ryan Coogler's Southern vampire drama 'Sinners' claimed four Academy Awards at the 98th Oscars ceremony on March 15, 2026, despite competing against fellow Warner Bros. film 'One Battle After Another,' which ultimately won Best Picture. The Four Wins: Best Original Screenplay went to director Ryan Coogler, who achieved Oscar-winning status with this achievement. The victory was widely anticipated by industry observers. Best Actor was awarded to Michael B. Jordan for his leading role, marking his first Oscar nomination and an unexpected upset victory during voting. He had emerged as a contender after gaining momentum at the Screen Actors Guild Awards. Best Cinematography made history when Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman to win in this category, overcoming competition from 'One Battle After Another's' Michael Bauman, who had won equivalent honors from BAFTA and the cinematography guild. Best Original Score went to Ludwig Göransson, securing his third Oscar and second for a Coogler film following 'Black Panther.' Record-Breaking Achievement: The film arrived at the ceremony with an unprecedented 16 nominations—more than any other film in Oscar history. While it won four awards, competing supporting nominees Wunmi Mosaku and Delroy Lindo did not prevail in their respective categories.
